summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service by expressing gratitude for the congregation and sharing a personal initiative to support a small church in Wickenburg, Arizona, during Pastor Appreciation Month. He emphasized that churches are teammates in God's kingdom, not competitors. The sermon, titled 'Cut It Out,' focused on John 15:1-8, where Jesus describes Himself as the true vine and the Father as the gardener who prunes branches to make them more fruitful. Pastor Travis explained that healthy spiritual production requires pruning, which involves cutting off dead things and cutting back living things that hinder our growth. He outlined four key areas of life that need pruning to bear more fruit for God. The message was a practical call to self-examination and intentional living, encouraging believers to remove hindrances so they can remain in Christ and live purposefully. He closed with an invitation for anyone to begin a relationship with Jesus.
mainPoints: Healthy spiritual production requires pruning, which means cutting off damaging things and cutting back good things that hinder our best growth.
We must guard our minds and cut out damaging thoughts by taking them captive and being mindful of what influences shape our thinking.
To live fruitfully, we need to prune our overcrowded schedules, self-defeating addictions, and unfruitful relationships that hold us back.

versesMentioned.text: I am the true vine, and my Father is the gardener. He cuts off every branch in me that bears no fruit, while every branch that does bear fruit he prunes so that it will be even more fruitful. You are already clean because of the word I have spoken to you. Remain in me, as I also remain in you. No branch can bear fruit by itself; it must remain in the vine. Neither can you bear fruit unless you remain in me. I am the vine; you are the branches. If you remain in me and I in you, you will bear much fruit; apart from me you can do nothing. If you do not remain in me, you are like a branch that is thrown away and withers; such branches are picked up, thrown into the fire and burned. If you remain in me and my words remain in you, ask whatever you wish, and it will be done for you. This is to my Father’s glory, that you bear much fruit, showing yourselves to be my disciples.
Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it.

For though we live in the world, we do not wage war as the world does. The weapons we fight with are not the weapons of the world. On the contrary, they have divine power to demolish strongholds. We demolish arguments and every pretension that sets itself up against the knowledge of God, and we take captive every thought to make it obedient to Christ.
For as he thinks in his heart, so is he.
Teach us to number our days, that we may gain a heart of wisdom.
Be very careful, then, how you live—not as unwise but as wise, making the most of every opportunity, because the days are evil.
I have the right to do anything,' you say—but not everything is beneficial. 'I have the right to do anything'—but I will not be mastered by anything.
So if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.
Do not be misled: 'Bad company corrupts good character.'
Do not be yoked together with unbelievers. For what do righteousness and wickedness have in common? Or what fellowship can light have with darkness?

Monday.scripture: John 15:5 (NIV) - 'I am the vine; you are the branches. If you remain in me and I in you, you will bear much fruit; apart from me you can do nothing.'
Monday.action: Spend 10 minutes in prayer, asking God to show you one area of your life that needs pruning to bear more fruit.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to remain in You today. Show me what I need to cut off or cut back to live a more fruitful life for Your glory.

Tuesday.scripture: Proverbs 4:23 (NIV) - 'Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it.'
Tuesday.action: Identify one negative or damaging thought pattern you have. Write down a biblical truth to replace it with.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, help me take my thoughts captive today. Guard my heart and mind from influences that do not honor You.

Wednesday.scripture: Psalm 90:12 (NIV) - 'Teach us to number our days, that we may gain a heart of wisdom.'
Wednesday.action: Look at your calendar for the next week. Intentionally block out one hour for rest, prayer, or time with family.
Wednesday.prayer: God, teach me to value my time. Help me create healthy margin so I can prioritize what truly matters.

Thursday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 6:12 (NIV) - 'I have the right to do anything,' you say—but not everything is beneficial. 'I have the right to do anything'—but I will not be mastered by anything.'
Thursday.action: Honestly assess if there is any habit or behavior that has mastery over you. Take one practical step to limit its influence today.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, I don't want to be mastered by anything but You. Give me strength and wisdom to break free from anything that controls me.

Friday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 15:33 (NIV) - 'Do not be misled: 'Bad company corrupts good character.''
Friday.action: Consider your closest relationships. Pray about whether any are holding you back spiritually and ask God for wisdom on how to set healthy boundaries.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, give me discernment in my relationships. Help me to build friendships that encourage my faith and growth in You.

Saturday.scripture: John 8:36 (NIV) - 'So if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.'
Saturday.action: Reflect on one area where you have experienced God's freedom this week. Share a sentence of gratitude with a friend or family member.
Saturday.prayer: Thank You, Jesus, for setting me free. Help me to walk in that freedom daily and to bear fruit that lasts for Your kingdom.
